description:
You are acting as the release-manager’s automation assistant.  
Inside the home directory an SQLite database already exists:

/home/user/releases/app_releases.db

The database has one table:

CREATE TABLE releases (
        version TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
        status  TEXT NOT NULL
);

Your goal: generate a one-line log file that tells how many releases are still waiting to be deployed.

1. Query the database for the number of rows whose  status  column equals the literal string “pending”.
2. Write that single integer to the file
   /home/user/release_logs/pending_count.log
   in the exact format shown below.  The automated grader will read this file and compare it byte-for-byte.

Required file content (assuming, as is currently true, there are two pending rows):

PENDING_RELEASES=2\n

• The word “PENDING_RELEASES” must appear in ALL-CAPS.  
• Exactly one “=” character must follow it.  
• The integer count must follow immediately with no extra spaces.  
• A single newline character (ASCII 0x0A) must terminate the line.  
• Nothing else may be written—no additional lines or whitespace.

Additional notes:

• The parent directory /home/user/release_logs may not exist yet; create it if necessary using only non-privileged commands.  
• Use any sqlite3 CLI options you wish, but accomplish the whole task with one terminal command if possible.  
• Do not modify the schema or the data in the database.  Only read from it.  
• When you have finished, the only artefact the grader will inspect is the exact content of /home/user/release_logs/pending_count.log as described above.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (before the agent starts):

Directories:
/home/user/releases               (already exists, mode 755, owned by user)
/home/user/release_logs           (does NOT exist)

SQLite database:
/home/user/releases/app_releases.db

SQLite schema inside app_releases.db:

CREATE TABLE releases (
        version TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
        status  TEXT NOT NULL
);

SQLite data inside releases table:
('v1.0.0',      'released')
('v1.1.0',      'released')
('v2.0.0',      'released')
('v2.1.0-beta', 'pending')
('v2.2.0',      'pending')

Therefore the correct pending count is integer 2.

Expected artefact after agent finishes:

File: /home/user/release_logs/pending_count.log  
Permissions: 644 (user-writable, world-readable)  
Exact byte content (11 bytes total):

50 45 4e 44 49 4e 47 5f 52 45 4c 45 41 53 45 53 3d 32 0a
ASCII representation:

PENDING_RELEASES=2\n
name: task_000000_d8a20cc5
